title: Tests — install side (snapshot + three-end propagation + Cursor migration + project-rules)
description: Rewrite the existing managed-block test block (install-skills-and-hooks.test.ts:442-574) for the new fabric:bootstrap marker, three per-client target paths, .cursor/rules directory migration, project-rules.md only-if-exists concat behavior, and snapshot write. Add a new bootstrap-snapshot.test.ts integration file covering .fabric/AGENTS.md write + idempotency. Mirrors patterns from codex-mcp-install.test.ts:146-171 (legacy migration) and install-diff-mode.test.ts.

files.change: Rewrite the four-axis matrix (presence × idempotency × in-place-replace × overwrite) for the new fabric:bootstrap marker. SECTION_BEGIN/SECTION_END at L442-L443 updated to '<!-- fabric:bootstrap:begin -->' / '<!-- fabric:bootstrap:end -->'. Test seed targets updated: AGENTS.md remains; CLAUDE.md remains; `.cursor/rules` (FILE) is replaced by `.cursor/rules/fabric-bootstrap.mdc` (DIRECTORY rule). New assertions: (1) .fabric/AGENTS.md exists after install with content byte-equal to BOOTSTRAP_CANONICAL; (2) Codex managed block body byte-equals .fabric/AGENTS.md content; (3) Cursor managed block body byte-equals same; (4) Cursor file front-matter has `alwaysApply: true`; (5) CLAUDE.md contains exactly one `@.fabric/AGENTS.md` line.

files.target: new test 'migrates legacy .cursor/rules flat-file to .cursor/rules/fabric-bootstrap.mdc directory rule'
files.change: Add test that pre-seeds `.cursor/rules` as a single file with legacy fabric:knowledge-base markers via writeFixtureFile, runs runInit, asserts: (1) flat `.cursor/rules` file deleted, (2) `.cursor/rules/fabric-bootstrap.mdc` created with new marker. Mirrors codex-mcp-install legacy-migration assertion shape.

Two-scenario test: (A) without project-rules.md — runInit, verify Codex managed block body byte-equals .fabric/AGENTS.md (no separator, no concat). (B) writeFixtureFile('.fabric/project-rules.md', 'CUSTOM RULES
'), runInit, verify Codex managed block body byte-equals .fabric/AGENTS.md + '
---
' + 'CUSTOM RULES
'. Both scenarios assert idempotent re-runs.

Update test that injects 'USER VANDALISM' string inside fabric:bootstrap markers, asserts vandalism wiped after re-install + canonical content restored + pre-section user content preserved. Same shape as existing test, just with new marker strings.

New file dedicated to TASK-02 snapshot writer + propagation byte-equality. Tests: (1) 'writes .fabric/AGENTS.md byte-equal to BOOTSTRAP_CANONICAL on first install', (2) 'second install is byte-identical (idempotent)', (3) 'restores deleted .fabric/AGENTS.md on re-install', (4) 'mkdirs .fabric/ parent when missing'. Use createWerewolfFixtureRoot + runInit pattern from init-test-utils.ts. Import BOOTSTRAP_CANONICAL from @fenglimg/fabric-shared.

Update marker constants to fabric:bootstrap. Add new assertions: (1) uninstall deletes .fabric/AGENTS.md (existsSync === false after uninstall); (2) uninstall preserves .fabric/project-rules.md if present (user-authored); (3) uninstall removes `@.fabric/AGENTS.md` line from CLAUDE.md; (4) uninstall deletes .cursor/rules/fabric-bootstrap.mdc managed block (or whole file).

implementation: Update SECTION_BEGIN/SECTION_END constants in install-skills-and-hooks.test.ts to new fabric:bootstrap marker
Update test seed target paths: `.cursor/rules` (file) → `.cursor/rules/fabric-bootstrap.mdc` (directory rule)
Add new assertions for .fabric/AGENTS.md byte equality with BOOTSTRAP_CANONICAL
Add new assertions for Codex/Cursor managed block body byte equality with snapshot content
Add new test for legacy .cursor/rules flat-file → directory-rule migration (clean-slate delete)
Add new two-scenario test for project-rules.md only-if-exists concat
Update vandalism test for new marker
Update uninstall test for new marker + new strip targets
Run `pnpm --filter=@fenglimg/fabric-cli test` and verify all tests pass

reference.pattern: install-skills-and-hooks.test.ts:442-574 four-axis matrix; codex-mcp-install.test.ts:146-171 legacy migration assertions; init-test-utils.ts helpers (createWerewolfFixtureRoot, runInit, snapshotTree, writeFixtureFile)
reference.examples: countOccurrences helper from install-skills-and-hooks.test.ts:445-455; regex match().length === 1 pattern from codex-mcp-install.test.ts:142-143 for 'exactly one marker pair' assertions

criteria: All updated tests in install-skills-and-hooks.test.ts pass (presence, idempotency, in-place-replace, vandalism — all with new fabric:bootstrap marker)
New test 'migrates legacy .cursor/rules flat-file to .cursor/rules/fabric-bootstrap.mdc' passes
New test 'project-rules.md only-if-exists concat' passes both A and B scenarios
New file bootstrap-snapshot.test.ts contains ≥ 4 tests, all passing
Updated uninstall-skills-and-hooks.test.ts passes (new marker + new strip behavior)
Test marker constants no longer reference fabric:knowledge-base anywhere (grep verification)
.cursor/rules path references in tests use `.cursor/rules/fabric-bootstrap.mdc` directory rule path
Tests cover the THREE new install steps from TASK-03 (bootstrap-claude, bootstrap-codex, bootstrap-cursor) plus the bootstrap-snapshot step from TASK-02

rationale.chosen_approach: Rewrite existing tests in-place + add new file for snapshot-specific behavior. Keeps the regression gate strong; ensures rc.19 changes don't silently break the install pipeline.
alternatives_considered: Add new file only, leave old tests in place (creates parallel test surfaces; old tests would fail on marker rename)
Delete old tests entirely (loses idempotency + vandalism coverage)
rationale.tradeoffs: Test churn vs additive-only. Acceptable; marker rename forces it.

manual_checks: Verify zero references to fabric:knowledge-base in test files post-edit
success_metrics: Test count increased by ≥ 6 (4 new in bootstrap-snapshot.test.ts + 2 new in install-skills-and-hooks.test.ts)
Zero references to legacy `.cursor/rules` flat-file path in test seed code (except in the new migration test)

risks.description: createWerewolfFixtureRoot may delete the pre-existing .cursor/ — need to confirm post-rc.19 fixture state allows seeding legacy .cursor/rules flat file
risks.mitigation: writeFixtureFile after createWerewolfFixtureRoot — explicit seeding always works regardless of fixture cleanup

risks.description: Tests may run in parallel and pollute each other if FABRIC_HOME not isolated (snapshot writer touches .fabric/ under target, not FABRIC_HOME — likely safe, but doctor-side tests in TASK-09 do touch FABRIC_HOME)
risks.mitigation: Install-side tests use target-scoped paths only; FABRIC_HOME isolation reserved for TASK-09 doctor tests
